scenario_results.reason: The package advances restoration state only when outage, switching, and field evidence are aligned and names the next action in utility-specific terms.
Missing damage-assessment and clearance records block progression and produce a specific exception summary instead of a guessed restoration state.
Conflicting OMS and field state is treated as an evidence conflict that pauses completion and requires reconciliation.
The specialist refuses completion without outage-specific closeout evidence and requests named closure artifacts.
Switching and re-energization authority are kept outside the lane and routed to the correct owner.
Critical-facility prioritization remains utility-specific and is escalated when the approved priority list is missing.
Mutual-aid handling requires concrete resource and staging artifacts before the package treats crews as restoration-ready.
Temporary repair approval is routed to engineering and safety ownership rather than absorbed by the restoration lane.
Conflicting emergency-plan and tenant rules are surfaced as low-confidence escalation rather than merged into one unsupported answer.
Nested-outage rework reverses lifecycle state cleanly and preserves completion traceability across both restoration records.
Generic field-service language is reframed into outage-restoration artifacts and blocked until utility-specific evidence is supplied.
External ETR and regulatory-commitment requests are refused unless approved policy and owner authority are present.

notes: All 12 scenarios pass with explicit outage-restoration terminology, lifecycle control, mutual-aid handling, and evidence-backed completion rules.
Functionality coverage is complete for all shared, execution-processing-and-fulfillment, and cross-industry ability requirements.
